hero.name: Nithun Manoharan
hero.intro: This is where I'll share what I am working on in my free time, completely unrelated to my professional life. Right now that is Deafening Silence, a tool for tinnitus sound therapy, and one that I hope to release for free as an app in the future. A shelf of books, games and TV I've enjoyed, and some of my writing — fiction or about the real world, whatever I have the time and interest for.
hero.teaser: The best is yet to come. Soon™
hero.donate: If you find anything here useful or enjoyable, I'd encourage you to donate to organisations doing real good: <0>Red Cross</0>, <1>Doctors Without Borders</1>, <2>Spire</2>, <3>The Development Fund</3>, <4>GiveWell</4> (global health), and the <5>hearing research charities listed on the Silence page</5>.

silence.heading: Deafening Silence
silence.subtitle: Deafening Silence is a small tool for tinnitus sound therapy. Tap a sound to play; adjust to your preference.
silence.guidance: Based on Tinnitus Retraining Therapy (TRT) principles: set the volume so the sound sits just below the level of your tinnitus — not to mask it completely. The goal is habituation over time, not suppression.
silence.donationIntro: If Deafening Silence helps you, please consider donating to research and advocacy. These organisations were chosen for being credible and focused on hearing loss and tinnitus research.
silence.audioError: Audio couldn't start. Please tap Play again.
silence.iosMuteNote: If you hear no sound, check that your mute switch is off. On iPhone, the side switch may silence audio even when volume is up.
